text:
In a sleepy seaside town, there lived a wise old owl named Ottley. Every evening, he'd whistle a song about oceans deep.

One sunny afternoon, young Mimmy decided to hear him. She squawked beside him.

"Why such deep things, owl?" she asked.

Ottley just hoot.

One night, stars glittered strangely.

"The weirdest thing," said Owl.

Could it be an owl moon?

They stared.

Then stars spun round.

A kite soared high.

Maybe it got lost? Owls think.

So they followed.

Stars lit up.

That kite was mine!

They found you.

On a cotton shelf.

Hawks won't hawk you.
